picks.reasoning: Liverpool at Anfield in a Champions League knockout tie is one of the most reliable home advantages in European football. Ladbrokes have this priced as almost a coin-flip (43.5% implied) which significantly underestimates Liverpool's strength at home in high-stakes UCL fixtures. PSG are a formidable side but have a poor record in away legs at elite Premier League atmospheres. The true probability of a Liverpool home win sits closer to 52%. At 2.3 this is clear value.

The defining context here is Simeone's home UCL knockout record: 17 games, 11 wins, 6 draws, 0 losses — he has never lost a home Champions League knockout match at the Metropolitano or Calderón. Ladbrokes price Barcelona as heavy away favorites at 1.8 (55.6% implied), essentially dismissing Atlético's home fortress status. The true probability for an Atlético win is closer to 35%. This is a significant market inefficiency driven by Barcelona's strong La Liga form obscuring Atlético's elite European home record.

Ipswich are priced as away favorites at 1.95 (51.3% implied) which seems excessive for a Championship away fixture at Fratton Park. Portsmouth's home support and the tight margins of Championship football make this more competitive than Ladbrokes suggest. The true probability for a Portsmouth home win is around 33% — a modest but genuine edge at 3.5. Minimum stake only.
